business_definition: The unique order — the spine every other table joins to. One row per order placed.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Spine key; no cleaning. Referential integrity verified (RI-00).
business_risk_if_misused: Joining to items/payments without aggregating to order grain first multiplies rows (fan-out).

technical_description: Per-order customer key (FK to customers; NOT the person).
business_definition: Per-order customer key, not a unique person identifier. Do not use for retention analysis.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Use customer_unique_id for any customer/retention/RFM metric — see DQ-03.
business_risk_if_misused: Counting customer_id as people zeroes out repeat rate and overstates customer count.

technical_description: delivered / shipped / canceled / unavailable / invoiced / processing / created / approved.
business_definition: Lifecycle state of the order; only 'delivered' should count toward net revenue and delivery KPIs.
allowed_values: delivered / shipped / canceled / unavailable / invoiced / processing / created / approved
treatment_cleaning_rule: Defines the 'delivered' flag; net revenue restricted to delivered (DQ-05).
business_risk_if_misused: Including non-delivered orders inflates net revenue and distorts delivery metrics.

business_definition: When the customer placed the order — clock-start for delivery time and basis for the monthly trend.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Parsed to datetime; basis for purchase_ym and delivery_days.
business_risk_if_misused: Parse/timezone errors shift the monthly trend and every delivery-day calculation.

business_definition: When payment was approved; the gap to purchase measures payment-processing lag.
treatment_cleaning_rule: 160 nulls tie to non-approved/canceled orders; excluded from approval-timing (DQ-05).
business_risk_if_misused: Treating nulls as zero-lag understates payment-processing time.

business_definition: When the parcel was handed to the carrier; separates seller handling from carrier transit.
treatment_cleaning_rule: 1,783 nulls = not yet shipped; excluded from carrier-time metrics (DQ-05).
business_risk_if_misused: Reading missing as an instant handoff understates seller/carrier handling time.

business_definition: When the customer actually received the order — end-point for delivery time, the strongest signal associated with satisfaction.
treatment_cleaning_rule: 2,965 nulls = not delivered; excluded from delivery KPIs (DQ-05).
business_risk_if_misused: Counting missing as on-time/zero-day understates delivery time and overstates the on-time rate.

business_definition: The delivery date promised to the customer at purchase; the benchmark for 'late'.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Promised date; basis for is_late / on-time rate.
business_risk_if_misused: A wrong benchmark flips is_late and misstates the on-time rate.

business_definition: Order this line item belongs to; the join back to the order spine (one order → many items).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Aggregate items to order grain before joining (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: A direct join multiplies product revenue by item count (~+4.5%).

business_definition: Line-item sequence number within an order (1, 2, 3…), not a quantity.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Counted per order → n_items, the fan-out indicator (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: Mistaking the max sequence for quantity miscounts units sold.

business_definition: Which product was bought on this line; links the sale to category and dimensions.
treatment_cleaning_rule: FK to products; integrity verified (RI-00).
business_risk_if_misused: A broken product join drops category revenue from the Pareto.

business_definition: Which seller fulfilled this line; supports seller-level operations analysis.
treatment_cleaning_rule: FK to sellers; no cleaning.
business_risk_if_misused: Multi-seller orders fan out if seller joins aren't aggregated.

business_definition: Deadline for the seller to hand the line to the carrier; a fulfilment SLA marker.
treatment_cleaning_rule: No cleaning; not used in headline KPIs.

technical_description: Item price (BRL); summed to GMV at order grain.
business_definition: Merchandise price of the line in BRL — the raw material of GMV.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Summed to order grain (order_product_revenue / GMV) before joining (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: Summing after a multi-grain join double-counts GMV (~+4.5%).

business_definition: Shipping charged on the line in BRL; tracked separately from merchandise revenue.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Summed to order_freight; reported separately from GMV.
business_risk_if_misused: Folding freight into GMV overstates merchandise revenue.

business_definition: Order this payment row settles; one order can have several payment/installment rows.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Aggregate payments to order grain before joining (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: A direct join multiplies cash collected (~+27%).

technical_description: Installment row sequence within the order.
business_definition: Sequence of the payment row within an order (installment/method split), not a separate sale.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Counted per order → n_payments, the fan-out indicator (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: Treating each row as a separate payment overstates cash and order count.

business_definition: How the customer paid; supports payment-mix analysis.
treatment_cleaning_rule: 3 'not_defined' rows flagged (DQ-08).
business_risk_if_misused: 'not_defined' rows skew payment-mix charts if not flagged.

business_definition: Number of installments the customer split the payment into (a Brazilian retail norm).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Max per order → n_installments.

business_definition: Cash amount on the payment row in BRL — the raw material of 'cash collected'.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Summed to order_payment_total; 9 rows ≤ 0 excluded from cash metrics (DQ-08).
business_risk_if_misused: Summing after a join inflates cash collected ~+27%; ≤0 rows distort totals.

business_definition: Unique satisfaction-survey record; occasionally more than one maps to an order.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Dedupe/aggregate to order grain before joining.
business_risk_if_misused: Duplicate reviews per order double-count if not reduced to order grain.

business_definition: Order the review is about; the join back to the order spine.
treatment_cleaning_rule: FK to orders; integrity verified (RI-00).

business_definition: Customer's 1–5 star rating — the headline satisfaction signal.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Used directly; mean per order feeds the score-vs-delivery analysis.
business_risk_if_misused: Averaging at the wrong grain or imputing missing biases satisfaction.

business_definition: Optional short title on the review; almost always blank — low analytical value.
treatment_cleaning_rule: 88.3% null; not used — see DQ-04.
business_risk_if_misused: Treating the 12% with a title as representative is selection bias.

business_definition: Optional free-text review — the voice-of-customer feeding the AI topic/sentiment step.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Scope text/AI analysis to the 40,977 with text; flag has_text, don't impute (DQ-04).
business_risk_if_misused: Generalizing the 41% who comment to all customers is selection bias.

business_definition: When the satisfaction survey was sent to the customer.
treatment_cleaning_rule: No cleaning.

business_definition: When the customer answered the survey.
treatment_cleaning_rule: No cleaning.

business_definition: Unique product in the catalog; links sales to category and dimensions.
treatment_cleaning_rule: FK target; integrity verified (RI-00).

business_definition: Product category in Portuguese — the basis for category revenue concentration.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Map to English; 610 nulls bucket to 'unknown' (DQ-06).
business_risk_if_misused: Dropping the 610 nulls hides a real revenue bucket; PT labels are unreadable to stakeholders.

business_definition: Character length of the product title (source typo preserved); a listing-quality proxy.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Descriptive only; nulls track the uncategorized products (DQ-06).

business_definition: Character length of the product description; a listing-quality proxy.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Descriptive only; nulls track the uncategorized products (DQ-06).

business_definition: Number of catalog photos on the listing; a listing-quality proxy.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Descriptive only; nulls track the uncategorized products (DQ-06).

business_definition: Product weight in grams; affects freight and dimensional analysis.
treatment_cleaning_rule: A couple of missing dimensions documented; not used in headline KPIs (DQ-06).
business_risk_if_misused: Outliers or missing weights distort any freight or size analysis.

business_definition: Package length in cm.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Missing dimensions documented; not used in headline KPIs (DQ-06).

business_definition: Package height in cm.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Missing dimensions documented; not used in headline KPIs (DQ-06).

business_definition: Package width in cm.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Missing dimensions documented; not used in headline KPIs (DQ-06).

business_definition: Unique marketplace seller.
treatment_cleaning_rule: FK target; no cleaning.

business_definition: Seller's postal-code prefix; locates the seller for logistics analysis.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Joins to deduped geolocation if a map is needed (DQ-02).
business_risk_if_misused: Joining to un-deduped geolocation explodes rows.

business_definition: Seller's city (free text, lowercase).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Free-text city names vary in spelling; no normalization applied.
business_risk_if_misused: Spelling variants split the same city across rows.

business_definition: Seller's 2-letter Brazilian state.
treatment_cleaning_rule: No cleaning.
business_risk_if_misused: Using seller_state for demand geography misattributes where customers are.

technical_description: Per-order customer key (FK to customers; NOT the person).
business_definition: Per-order customer key, not a unique person identifier. Do not use for retention analysis.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Use customer_unique_id for any customer/retention/RFM metric — see DQ-03.
business_risk_if_misused: Counting customer_id as people zeroes out repeat rate and overstates customer count.

technical_description: Real person key — used for RFM/repeat (99,441 ids -> 96,096).
business_definition: The stable person key — the correct identifier for repeat rate, RFM and any customer count.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Key RFM/repeat on this field; 99,441 order keys → 96,096 people (DQ-03).

business_definition: Customer's postal-code prefix; locates the buyer for logistics/geo analysis.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Joins to deduped geolocation if a map is needed (DQ-02).
business_risk_if_misused: Joining to un-deduped geolocation explodes rows ~53×.

business_definition: Customer's city (free text, lowercase).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Free-text city names vary in spelling; no normalization applied.
business_risk_if_misused: Spelling variants split the same city across rows.

business_definition: Customer's 2-letter Brazilian state — the basis for revenue-by-state (São Paulo = 38%).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Used directly for revenue-by-state, avoiding the geolocation join entirely.
business_risk_if_misused: Using seller_state or geolocation instead misattributes demand geography.

business_definition: Postal-code prefix; the key the location table is collapsed to.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Dedupe 1,000,163 rows to one per prefix: mean lat/lng, modal city/state (DQ-02).
business_risk_if_misused: Joining un-deduped multiplies rows ~53× and corrupts any geo aggregate.

business_definition: Latitude ping for the prefix; averaged to one point per prefix after dedupe.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Mean latitude per prefix after dedupe (DQ-02).
business_risk_if_misused: Raw rows over-weight prefixes that have more pings.

business_definition: Longitude ping for the prefix; averaged to one point per prefix after dedupe.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Mean longitude per prefix after dedupe (DQ-02).
business_risk_if_misused: Raw rows over-weight prefixes that have more pings.

business_definition: City for the prefix (free text); reduced to the most common value per prefix.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Modal city per prefix after dedupe (DQ-02).
business_risk_if_misused: Spelling variants and multi-city prefixes blur city-level maps.

business_definition: State for the prefix; reduced to the most common value per prefix.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Modal state per prefix after dedupe (DQ-02).

business_definition: Portuguese category label — the lookup key to its English name.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Lookup key; 71 of 73 categories present (DQ-07).
business_risk_if_misused: Two PT categories missing here drop out of the English Pareto if not hand-added.

business_definition: English category label shown to stakeholders in the category Pareto.
treatment_cleaning_rule: 2 untranslated categories get manual labels: gaming_pc, portable_kitchen_and_food_preparers (DQ-07).
business_risk_if_misused: Missing manual labels drop pc_gamer / portable-kitchen revenue from the Pareto.

technical_description: Sum of items.price per order (GMV contribution).
business_definition: Merchandise revenue (GMV contribution) for the order = Σ item prices, computed before any join.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Σ items.price at order grain (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: If recomputed from a fanned-out join, GMV inflates ~+4.5%.

business_definition: Total shipping for the order = Σ item freight; reported separately from GMV.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Σ items.freight_value at order grain.
business_risk_if_misused: Folding into GMV overstates merchandise revenue.

business_definition: Total order value = merchandise + freight; the order-grain figure used for AOV and reconciliation.
treatment_cleaning_rule: order_product_revenue + order_freight, per order (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: Double-counts if derived after a multi-grain join.

technical_description: Item count per order (fan-out indicator; max 21).
business_definition: Items on the order — the fan-out indicator that proves the join-grain risk (max 21).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Count of item rows per order (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: Misread as 'quantity sold' rather than a fan-out diagnostic.

business_definition: Cash collected on the order = Σ payment rows; matches order_value almost exactly (r = 1.000).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Σ payments.payment_value at order grain (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: Inflates ~+27% if summed after joining payments directly.

business_definition: Payment/installment rows on the order — the cash-side fan-out indicator (max 29).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Count of payment rows per order (DQ-01).
business_risk_if_misused: Misread as separate payments overstates cash and transaction count.

business_definition: Largest installment count on the order; a credit-behavior signal.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Max installments across the order's payment rows.

technical_description: Payment type of the largest payment row.
business_definition: Payment method of the order's largest payment row; the order's headline method.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Type of the largest payment row per order.
business_risk_if_misused: A single-method label hides mixed-method orders.

business_definition: Whether the order reached the customer; the gate for net revenue and delivery KPIs.
treatment_cleaning_rule: order_status == 'delivered' (DQ-05).
business_risk_if_misused: Net revenue/AOV are wrong if undelivered orders aren't excluded.

business_definition: Days from purchase to delivery — the operational signal most associated with low scores (median 10.2 d).
treatment_cleaning_rule: delivered_customer − purchase; null when not delivered (DQ-05).
business_risk_if_misused: Counting nulls as 0 understates delivery time and the r = −0.33 signal.

business_definition: Promised delivery time in days (estimate − purchase); the benchmark behind is_late.
treatment_cleaning_rule: estimated − purchase.

business_definition: Whether delivery missed the promised date; the on-time / late flag.
treatment_cleaning_rule: delivered_customer > estimated_delivery_date (DQ-05).
business_risk_if_misused: Computed on missing delivery dates it gives a false on-time rate.

business_definition: Year-month of purchase; the time axis for the monthly trend (peak Nov-2017 = 7,289).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Year-month bucket of order_purchase_timestamp.

business_definition: Mean review score for the order; the satisfaction measure joined to delivery time.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Mean of review_score per order.
business_risk_if_misused: Missing ≠ neutral; imputing biases satisfaction.

technical_description: 1 if any review has comment text.
business_definition: Flag for whether the order's review carried free text; scopes the voice-of-customer sample.
treatment_cleaning_rule: 1 if any review on the order has comment text (DQ-04).

business_definition: Stakeholder-readable English category; the dimension behind the 18-of-74 concentration finding.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Mapped from PT via the translation table; nulls bucketed 'unknown' (DQ-06/DQ-07).
business_risk_if_misused: Dropping 'unknown' hides ~1.85% of products and their revenue.

technical_description: Days since last purchase (ref = max purchase date).
business_definition: Days since the customer's last purchase (vs the dataset's max date); the R in RFM.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Per customer_unique_id; reference = max purchase date (DQ-03).
business_risk_if_misused: Keyed on customer_id instead of unique_id makes everyone look like a one-time buyer.

business_definition: Distinct orders per person; the F in RFM and the basis for repeat rate (3.12%).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Distinct orders per customer_unique_id (DQ-03).
business_risk_if_misused: Keyed on customer_id zeroes out the repeat rate.

business_definition: Total spend per person; the M in RFM.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Σ order_value per customer_unique_id (DQ-03).
business_risk_if_misused: A wrong key splits one person's spend across multiple order ids.

business_definition: Recency quintile (5 = most recent); part of the transparent RFM rule.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Quintile of recency_days.

business_definition: Monetary quintile (5 = highest spend); part of the RFM rule.
treatment_cleaning_rule: Quintile of monetary.

business_definition: Whether the person ordered more than once; the headline retention flag (3.12% true).
treatment_cleaning_rule: frequency ≥ 2 on customer_unique_id (DQ-03).
business_risk_if_misused: Keyed on customer_id this collapses to ~0% — a fabricated 'no repeats' story.

business_definition: Plain-language RFM segment for targeting (e.g., new, loyal, at-risk).
treatment_cleaning_rule: Transparent rule over R/M scores and repeat status.
business_risk_if_misused: Opaque segment rules undermine stakeholder trust.

business_definition: AI-assigned theme of the review; feeds the negative-review breakdown (product 52% / delivery 38%).
treatment_cleaning_rule: LLM label validated vs an independent 2nd LLM annotator: κ = 0.81 (DQ-10).
business_risk_if_misused: Treating sample labels as a full-population count over-claims precision.

business_definition: AI-assigned sentiment of the review text; cross-checked against the star rating it never saw.
treatment_cleaning_rule: LLM label validated: κ = 0.89, 82.8% star agreement (DQ-10).
business_risk_if_misused: Treating labels as ground truth ignores the validated error rate.
